results.title: NotebookLM Custom Infographic Styles: Turn any source into a styled infographic | Product Hunt
results.content:
### AI Agents

### Trending categories

### Top reviewed

### Trending products

### Top forum threads

Krisp Voice Translation

What do you think? …

Rohan Chaubey

Visual communication has always been the bottleneck nobody talks about.

You do the research. You synthesize it with AI. Then you paste it into Canva, fight with layouts, pick fonts, give up, and ship something mediocre. The insight was great. The visual killed it. 😮‍💨

NotebookLM just quietly patched that gap. 🔧

Custom Infographic Styles gives you 10 visual presets plus a fully custom mode to transform any source material into a publication-ready infographic. One click. Source-grounded, not hallucinated. ✅

The styles are genuinely varied:

🌸 Kawaii for approachable, shareable content

Using the Othership app has transformed my wellness routine. With over 500 music-driven breathwork sessions, it’s easy to find what I need, whether I want to boost my energy or unwind before sleep. The blend of music and guided breathing is immersive and effective, helping me focus and regulate my emotions. Plus, the science-backed techniques deepen my understanding of breathwork. Overall, Othership is user-friendly and versatile—perfect for anyone looking to enhance their mental health. I highly recommend it!

results.title: Nano Banana 2: Google's latest AI image generation model  | Product Hunt
results.content:
### Trending products

### Top forum threads

Customer.io

What do you think? …

Rohan Chaubey

Major update from @Google: Nano Banana 2 🍌 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image). It’s state-of-the-art for creating and editing images, combining Pro-level capabilities with lightning-fast speed.

You now get:

Advanced world knowledge powered by Gemini

Real-time web search grounding for accurate subject rendering

Rapid edits and iteration with Gemini Flash speed

This isn’t just faster images, it’s context-aware generation.

What's new?

Stronger instruction following: Better adherence to complex prompts means fewer retries and more predictable outputs critical for production workflows.

results.title: Moda: Finally, AI designs you can edit | Product Hunt
results.content:
Ravi Parikh
Moda

Moda

@pidsinee\_einarsdottir yes, that's 100% the pain point we wanted to solve. Nano Banana and other image models are great but they give you a raster output that's not editable unless you re-prompt, and then you rarely get exactly what you want when you do that!

soubangi rajkhowa

Finally, an AI design tool that doesn't just give me a flat image! As an HR professional, I love the ability to tweak every layer. However, the next frontier for Moda should be better data-grounding.. We need the AI to be as smart with our provided info as it is with the layout. Great launch, team—this is a huge step forward for editable AI!

Anvisha Pai
Moda

Moda [...] So we did something that most teams wouldn't attempt.

We built a powerful WebGPU canvas from scratch, engineered from the ground up for people and agents to work together. Then we taught an agent layout, typography, and color. We taught it taste.

Moda is a brand-aligned design agent that produced fully editable designs with layers on a canvas that you control.

⚡ What people are making:

Pitch decks, QBR and sales slides

LinkedIn and Instagram posts with animations

Sales collateral and one-pagers

Ads, UI mockups, even printed large-format designs for booths and live events

💬 Early users say Moda already beats Canva, Gamma, and dedicated contractors: [...] Ravi Parikh
Moda

Moda

@anvisha\_pai  @arcinston great idea - we're rapidly building out integrations now. You can export/import from Google Slides, PowerPoint, standard raster formats (PNG, JPG, etc), MP4/GIF for videos, and we're adding a lot more!

Nastassia 
Scade.pro

Scade.pro

@anvisha\_pai congrats on the launch! amazing demo. It feels like a really simple-to-use product

Anvisha Pai
Moda

Moda

@nastassia\_k thank you!

Kimberly Ross

@anvisha\_pai Congrats on your launch! This is so innovative. How does Moda prevent prompt drift from degrading layout quality over multiple iterations? Is there an internal representation of layout intent vs surface styling?

Mahesh Yadav
